query: done with this programme, how do i actually bail out officially — is not paying the fee enough or do i have to do something

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
retrieved.text: / - I have paid a too high tuition fee. Will it be reimbursed automatically? If you have paid a too high tuition fee you have to apply for reimbursement yourself. The remaining difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - I have closed my degree programme before the end of the admission period. Will the tuition fee be reimbursed? You have closed your degree programme before the end of the admission period of the current semester and you are not admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - I have completed my degree programme. Will my tuition fee be reimbursed? If you complete your degree programme until 31 October in winter semester and are also not admitted to any other programme where you have to pay the tuition fee, the fee can be reimbursed. This also the case if you have already taken the last examination but the grade is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is 31 October at the latest. The same is applies

You have paid via eps-payment and the fee was deducted twice by the bank. Please contact your bank. It has to directly get in touch with the Raiffeisenbank (bank connection of the University of Vienna). A return remittance is only possible via the Raiffeisenbank. → Solution: Directly contact your bank. - An error occurred during the payment of my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. What can I do? In case of an incorrect payment the fee will be returned to your account in most cases. You have to pay again correctly and within the payment period. Then you can re-enrol in your degree programme. You can find possible reasons for an incorrect payment and solutions for the problem when you look at the question "My fee does not register as received..." - I have already paid my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Why can't I download my student documents? If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee via payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union fee has been paid to the University of Vienna. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
heading_path: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
retrieved.text:
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at another university
retrieved.text: If you study at multiple universities in Austria, you can decide where you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee yourself. You have already paid at another university? Then you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself each semester. Otherwise your studies will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. / - How do I re-enrol in my studies if I pay at another university? 1. Pay the prescribed fee at the other university and wait for a few days. 2. Log into u:space within the payment period of the University of Vienna. 3. Verify if the fee is already marked as "received" at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. There may be a remaining difference left to pay. This may be the case if you have, for example, paid at an Austrian university of applied science but the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna. 4. Look for the degree programme you want to continue studying at Studies > Study overview. Confirm that you want to continue studying this degree programme. To do so choose the button "continue studying". If you want to continue studying multiple degree programmes you have to repeat this step for each programme respectively. 5. Refresh the page in u:space. In the study overview you can now see that you are re-enroled in your degree

In this case the [admission to a non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-qualitative-admission-requirements/) is possible. You will be admitted to the non-degree programme for the purpose of establishing equivalency. In the non-degree programme you pay the tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: Reimbursement of the tuition fee Already paid tuition fee can be reimbursed for certain, precisley defined reasons. This is the case, for example, if your application for remission or leave of absence has been granted. Further examples: You have completed your studies before the end of the admission period or you have paid too much. The application for remission is granted, but you have already paid the tuition fee? Read the information on tuition fee reimbursement and note the application deadlines. retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
retrieved.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

You can find possible reasons for an incorrect payment and solutions for the problem when you look at the question "My fee does not register as received..." - I have already paid my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Why can't I download my student documents? If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee via payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

If you also have to pay the tuition gee, you have to pay it within the payment period. Detailed information about the payment options is available in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.

You have paid a too high/too little fee. Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union fee has been paid to the University of Vienna. If you pay at an FH: - Pay the prescribed fee. - Wait for a week. - Check if the Students' Union fee has been marked as "received" in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. - If this is the case you can [re-enrol in your degree programme](#c1123247) at Studies > Study overview. Do not forget: If the tuition fee is also prescribed for you at the University of Vienna you also have to pay the remaining difference. - Should I pay with some kind of express payment, so the money is transferred faster? No, this is not necessary. The transfer is not much faster that way. An express payment does not really affect the process. The fastest way is paying directly via eps-payment (only possible with Austrian bank account) or credit card in u:space. In this case the fee is marked as "received" on the same day. - Can I pay my tuition fee/Students' Union fee in advance for the next semester? This is not possible. You can only pay the tuition fee/Students'

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
heading_path: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
reranked.text:
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

reranked.text: If you study at multiple universities in Austria, you can decide where you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee yourself. You have already paid at another university? Pay the prescribed fee at the other university and wait for a few days. 2. Log into u:space within the payment period of the University of Vienna. 3. Verify if the fee is already marked as "received" at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. There may be a remaining difference left to pay. This may be the case if you have, for example, paid at an Austrian university of applied science but the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna. 4. Look for the degree programme you want to continue studying at Studies > Study overview. Confirm that you want to continue studying this degree programme. To do so choose the button "continue studying". If you want to continue studying multiple degree programmes you have to repeat this step for each programme respectively. 5. Refresh the page in u:space. In the study overview you can now see that you are re-enroled in your degree

In the non-degree programme you pay the tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee. - Making up for qualitative admission requirements (QZB) You have to make up examinations to fulfil the QZB? In this case the [admission to a non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-qualitative-admission-requirements/) is possible. In the non-degree programme you pay the tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee. - Nostrification Detailed info about the prerequisites and course of the nostrification is available on the website of the [Büro Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/). You will be admitted to the non-degree programme for the purpose of establishing equivalency. In the non-degree programme you pay the tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee.

The application for remission is granted, but you have already paid the tuition fee? Read the information on tuition fee reimbursement and note the application deadlines. Please enquire about this topic at an Austrian representation authority (embassy, consulate) or the Municipal Department 35 (MA 35). A residence permit can also have an influence on the amount of

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
reranked.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

selected.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
selected.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

selected.title: Remission and reimbursement
selected.text: / - I have paid a too high tuition fee. Will it be reimbursed automatically? If you have paid a too high tuition fee you have to apply for reimbursement yourself. The remaining difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - I have closed my degree programme before the end of the admission period. Will the tuition fee be reimbursed? You have closed your degree programme before the end of the admission period of the current semester and you are not admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - I have completed my degree programme. Will my tuition fee be reimbursed? If you complete your degree programme until 31 October in winter semester and are also not admitted to any other programme where you have to pay the tuition fee, the fee can be reimbursed. This also the case if you have already taken the last examination but the grade is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is 31 October at the latest. The same is applies

Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. selected.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
selected.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

selected.title: ABC of terminology
selected.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

assemble.context:
[1] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — FAQ
completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

[2] Remission and reimbursement — FAQ about the reimbursement
/ - I have paid a too high tuition fee. Will it be reimbursed automatically? If you have paid a too high tuition fee you have to apply for reimbursement yourself. The remaining difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - I have closed my degree programme before the end of the admission period. Will the tuition fee be reimbursed? You have closed your degree programme before the end of the admission period of the current semester and you are not admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - I have completed my degree programme. Will my tuition fee be reimbursed? If you complete your degree programme until 31 October in winter semester and are also not admitted to any other programme where you have to pay the tuition fee, the fee can be reimbursed. This also the case if you have already taken the last examination but the grade is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is 31 October at the latest. The same is applies

[3] Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment) — FAQ
the case, the bank cannot allocate the fee. It will return the fee to the bank location the payment was made from. → Solution: Directly contact the bank location you paid at. 6. You have paid via eps-payment and the fee was deducted twice by the bank. Please contact your bank. It has to directly get in touch with the Raiffeisenbank (bank connection of the University of Vienna). A return remittance is only possible via the Raiffeisenbank. → Solution: Directly contact your bank. - An error occurred during the payment of my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. What can I do? In case of an incorrect payment the fee will be returned to your account in most cases. You have to pay again correctly and within the payment period. Then you can re-enrol in your degree programme. You can find possible reasons for an incorrect payment and solutions for the problem when you look at the question "My fee does not register as received..." - I have already paid my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Why can't I download my student documents? If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee via payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the

[4] Leave of absence — FAQ
after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

[5] Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.
